{{ill|Nagare-zukuri|lt=nagare-zukuri|en|Nagare-zukuri|fr|Nagare-zukuri|it|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造|pl|Nagare-zukuri|ru|Нагарэ-дзукури|sv|Nagare-zukuri|zh|流造|WD=Q210684}} == country (P17) == * {{ill|Q17|lt=Q17|WD=Q17}} == name in kana (P1814) == * こじのじんじゃ == has part(s) (P527) == * {{ill|Q135018062|lt=Q135018062|WD=Q135018062}} ** P1114: {'amount': '+2', 'unit': '1'} * {{ill|Kojino Shrine Kamisha|lt=Kojino Shrine Kamisha|WD=Q135193515}} ** P1352: {'amount': '+1', 'unit': '1'} ** P3831: {{ill|Q135022904|lt=Q135022904|WD=Q135022904}} * {{ill|Q135193516|lt=Q135193516|WD=Q135193516}}Kokugakuin University Shrine database ** P1352: {'amount': '+2', 'unit': '1'} ** P3831: {{ill|Q135022904|lt=Q135022904|WD=Q135022904}} == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '滋賀県守山市洲本町字開発1607', 'language': 'ja'} * {'text': '滋賀県守山市洲本町開発／中の前', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:己爾乃神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11479874}} == Japanese content == '''己爾乃神社'''（こじのじんじゃ）は{{jalink|滋賀県|Q131358}}{{jalink|守山市|Q857225}}洲本町に鎮座する{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}である。『{{jalink|延喜式神名帳|Q11064932}}』{{jalink|野洲郡|Q8049933|近江国野洲郡}}に「己尓乃神社 二座」と載せる{{jalink|式内社}}『神名帳』は「尓」字を「人」の下に「小」と作る。また、訓を付ける九条家本は「己尓乃」を「コニノ」と訓んでいる。。 およそ500{{jalink|メートル|Q11573}}を隔てて同名の神社が2社存し、埋め立てられたがかつては{{jalink|野洲川|Q8049935}}から分かれて{{jalink|琵琶湖|Q200239}}にそそぐ野洲川南流が流れており、ともにその左岸堤防上に位置していたため、河川に対する信仰に発するものと思われる『式内社調査報告』。。また、ともに{{jalink|式内社}}の伝承を有すが、いずれかをそれと決することは地理的、史料的にも不可能で、むしろ2社を以て構成されたのが式内「己尓乃神社」であり、かつては祭祀においても密接な繋がりを持つものであった可能性が指摘されている。 === 己爾乃神社（開発） === {{神社 |名称 = 己爾乃神社 |画像 = [[File:己爾乃神社 - panoramio.jpg|270px]]
鳥居 |所在地 = {{jalink|滋賀県|Q131358}}{{jalink|守山市|Q857225}}洲本町字開発1607 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|05|37.4|N|135|58|12.7|E|scale:25000}} |祭神 = {{jalink|伊邪那岐命}}
{{jalink|伊邪那美命}} |社格 = {{jalink|式内社}}論社・旧{{jalink|村社|Q134917284}} |創建 = 伝・{{jalink|天長|Q1373138}}6年（829年） |本殿 = {{jalink|流造|Q210684|一間社流造}} |別名 = 開発の己爾乃神社 |札所等 = |例祭 = {{jalink|5月5日|Q2550}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Shiga|label=己爾乃神社|coordinates= {{coord2|35|05|37.4|N|135|58|12.7|E|}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|05|37.4|N|135|58|12.7|E}}}} }} 洲本町字開発に鎮座。鎮座地から「開発の己爾乃神社」と称されるが、埋め立て前の野洲川南流に「列系図橋」という橋が架かっていて、その近くに位置していたことから「列系図の己爾乃神社」とも呼ばれていた。 === 祭神 === {{jalink|イザナギ|Q813858|伊邪那岐命}}と{{jalink|イザナミ|Q682306|伊邪那美命}}を主祭神に、{{jalink|天児屋命|Q2564508|天児屋根命}}と{{jalink|伊香津臣命}}を配祀する。 古くは配祀2柱を、天児屋根命は'''{{jalink|若一王子|Q11616186}}'''、伊香津臣命は'''{{jalink|若一権現}}'''と称して主祭神としていたが、{{jalink|明治|Q3125096}}14年（{{jalink|1881年|Q7808}}）9月22日付で現在のように変更された。 === 由緒 === 社伝に、{{jalink|天長|Q1373138}}6年（{{jalink|829年|Q30357}}）に伊香厚武が創建したとする。厚武は「大夫厚武」とも称し、伊香宿祢豊厚を祖とする{{jalink|伊香氏|Q135005986}}の末孫であるといい、伊香氏は{{jalink|中臣氏|Q1198447}}の同族なので『{{jalink|新撰姓氏録|Q1045362}}』左京神別上。、中臣氏の祖神である天児屋根命と伊香津臣命を奉斎したものという滋賀県神社庁、「[http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=463 己爾乃神社]」（平成22年2月28日閲覧）。。しばしば野洲川の氾濫に見舞われたため、『己爾乃神社之縁由書』という1冊を残して神宝や古記録を流失している。また{{jalink|若一王寺 (守山市)||若一王寺}}という{{jalink|神宮寺|Q1074971}}もあり、その跡を継ぐのが近在の{{jalink|蓮光寺 (守山市)||蓮光寺}}であるが、『{{jalink|近江輿地志略|Q85883966}}』には神社付近に伝教大師（{{jalink|最澄|Q449943}}）作という仏像を安置する{{jalink|薬師堂|Q11622438}}があったと記すので、それが{{jalink|本地仏}}を祀っていたものと推測される。後に{{jalink|神祇管領}}の{{jalink|吉田家|Q11413722}}より正一位の{{jalink|宗源宣旨}}を受け、現に「正一位若一大権現」と記された神鏡を伝えている。{{jalink|旧社格}}は{{jalink|村社|Q134917284}}。 === 祭事 === * 例祭 - 5月5日 === 境内社 === * 巳爾乃若宮 === 己爾乃神社（中の前） === {{神社 |名称 = 己爾乃神社 |画像 = [[File:Kojino-jinja (Nakanomae) 202202-1.jpg|270px]]
鳥居 |所在地 = {{jalink|滋賀県|Q131358}}{{jalink|守山市|Q857225}}洲本町字中の前2322 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|05|42.0|N|135|57|56.7|E|scale:25000}} |祭神 = {{jalink|大己貴命}}
{{jalink|天津彦根命}}
{{jalink|素盞鳴命}} |社格 = {{jalink|式内社}}論社・旧{{jalink|村社|Q134917284}} |創建 = 伝・{{jalink|大宝|Q423370}}3年（703年） |本殿 = {{jalink|流造|Q210684|一間社流造}} |別名 = 大曲の己爾乃神社 |札所等 = |例祭 = {{jalink|5月5日|Q2550}} |神事 = 名付祭 |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Shiga|label=己爾乃神社|coordinates= {{coord2|35|05|42.0|N|135|57|56.7|E|}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|05|42.0|N|135|57|56.7|E}}}} }} 洲本町字中の前に鎮座。鎮座地から「中の前の己爾乃神社」や、「大曲の己爾乃神社」と呼ばれる（大曲（おおまがり）は中の前に隣接するかつての大字名）。 === 祭神 === {{jalink|大国主|Q276944|大己貴命}}、{{jalink|アマツヒコネ|Q10940228|天津彦根命}}、{{jalink|スサノオ|Q272993|素盞嗚命}}を祀る。 === 由緒 === {{jalink|大宝 (日本)|Q1346269|大宝}}3年（{{jalink|703年|Q23500}}）に{{jalink|中臣為恵}}により創祀されたと伝わる。{{jalink|永禄|Q1143341}}年間（1558-70年）の兵乱により社殿等を失ったが、{{jalink|寛政|Q1195131}}3年（{{jalink|1791年|Q7784}}）には旧に復した。近在の{{jalink|玉林寺 (守山市)||玉林寺}}がかつての神宮寺であった。旧社格は村社。 === 祭事 === * 例祭 - 5月5日 === 境内社 === * 稲荷神社 * 天満宮 * 大城神社 === 文化財 === * 銅製経筒 - 高さ25.3{{jalink|センチメートル|Q174728}}、口径11.2センチメートル。{{jalink|室町時代|Q334845}}の{{jalink|銅|Q753|銅製}}{{jalink|経筒|Q11606503}}。{{jalink|昭和|Q327567}}49年（{{jalink|1974年|Q2478}}）8月26日に守山市の文化財（工芸）に指定。 === 脚注 === {{reflist}} === 参考文献 === *『式内社調査報告』第12巻東山道1、{{jalink|皇學館大學||皇學館大學出版部}}、昭和56年 === 外部リンク === *[http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=463 己爾乃神社]（開発）（滋賀県神社庁） *[http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=464 己爾乃神社]（中の前）（滋賀県神社庁） {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:こしのしんしや}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Buddhism-stub}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{pref-stub|pref=滋賀県}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kojino Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kojino Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Kojino Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Disputed Shikinaisha or Shikigeisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ōmi Province]] [[Category:滋賀県の神社]] [[Category:近江国の式内社]] [[Category:滋賀県の旧村社]] [[Category:守山市の歴史]] [[Category:守山市の建築物]] [[Category:熊野信仰]] [[Category:need translation]]

'calendarmodel': 'http://www.wikidata.org/entity/Q1985727'} ** P1534: {{ill|Q3029647|lt=Q3029647|WD=Q3029647}} == architectural style (P149) == * Kamisha|lt=Kojino Shrine Kamisha|WD=Q135193515}} ** P1352: {'amount': '+1', 'unit': '1'} ** P3831: {{ill|Q135022904|lt=Q135022904|WD=Q135022904}} * {{ill|Q135193516|lt=Q135193516|WD=Q135193516}}Kokugakuin University Shrine database ** P1352: {'amount': '+2', 'unit': '1'} ** P3831: {{ill|Q135022904|lt=Q135022904|WD=Q135022904}} == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '滋賀県守山市洲本町字開発1607', 'language': 'ja'} * {'text': '滋賀県守山市洲本町開発／中の前', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:己爾乃神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11479874}} == Japanese content == '''Kojino Shrine''' (こじのじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} in Sumoto-cho, {{jalink|Moriyama|Q857225}}, {{jalink|Shiga Prefecture|Q131358}}. In the ''{{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyocho|Q11064932}}'', under {{jalink|Yasu District|Q8049933|Yasu District of Omi Province}}, it is listed as "Kojino Shrine, two seats," and is a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}}In the ''Jinmyocho'', the character for "ko" is written with a graph formed by "small" under "person." The Kujo-family annotated text reads "Kojino" as "Konino.". Two shrines with the same name exist about 500 {{jalink|meters|Q11573}} apart. Before land reclamation, the south branch of the {{jalink|Yasu River|Q8049935}} flowing from the Yasu River to {{jalink|Lake Biwa|Q200239}} ran between them, and both stood on the left-bank levee, so they are thought to originate in river-related worship''Shikinaisha Chosa Hokoku''.. Both preserve traditions of being the Shikinaisha, but it is geographically and historically impossible to determine one as the sole original shrine; rather, it has been pointed out that the Engishiki "Kojino Shrine" may have consisted of both shrines, which may once have had close ritual ties. === Kojino Shrine (Kaihatsu) === {{神社 |名称 = Kojino Shrine |画像 = [[File:己爾乃神社 - panoramio.jpg|270px]]
Torii |所在地 = {{jalink|Shiga Prefecture|Q131358}}{{jalink|Moriyama|Q857225}} Sumoto-cho, Kaihatsu 1607 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|05|37.4|N|135|58|12.7|E|scale:25000}} |祭神 = {{jalink|Izanagi-no-Mikoto}}
{{jalink|Izanami-no-Mikoto}} |社格 = {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} candidate shrine / former {{jalink|village shrine|Q134917284}} |創建 = tradition: {{jalink|Tencho|Q1373138}} 6 (829) |本殿 = {{jalink|nagare-zukuri|Q210684|one-bay nagare-zukuri}} |別名 = Kaihatsu no Kojino Shrine |札所等 = |例祭 = {{jalink|May 5|Q2550}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Shiga|label=Kojino Shrine|coordinates= {{coord2|35|05|37.4|N|135|58|12.7|E|}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|05|37.4|N|135|58|12.7|E}}}} }} It is located in Kaihatsu, Sumoto-cho. From its location it is called "Kaihatsu no Kojino Shrine," but because a bridge called "Retsukeizu-bashi" once crossed the former south branch of the Yasu River nearby before reclamation, it was also called "Retsukeizu no Kojino Shrine". === Enshrined deities === It enshrines {{jalink|Izanagi|Q813858|Izanagi-no-Mikoto}} and {{jalink|Izanami|Q682306|Izanami-no-Mikoto}} as principal deities, with {{jalink|Ame-no-Koyane|Q2564508|Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto}} and {{jalink|Ikatsuchi no Omi no Mikoto}} as associated deities. Formerly the two associated deities were the principal deities under the names '''{{jalink|Wakaichioji|Q11616186}}''' (for Ame-no-Koyane) and '''{{jalink|Wakaichi Gongen}}''' (for Ikatsuchi-no-Omi), but on September 22, {{jalink|1881|Q7808}} (Meiji 14), they were changed to the current arrangement. === Origin === Shrine tradition says it was founded in {{jalink|829|Q30357}} ({{jalink|Tencho|Q1373138}} 6) by Iga Atsumu. Atsumu, also called "Taifu Atsumu," is said to be a descendant of the Iga clan, whose ancestor was Iga no Suketoyo. Since the Iga clan was related to the {{jalink|Nakatomi clan|Q1198447}}''{{jalink|Shinsen Shojiroku|Q1045362}}'', Sakyo Shinbetsu-jō., it is said they enshrined Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto and Ikatsuchi-no-Omi-no-Mikoto, ancestral deities of the Nakatomi clanShiga Prefecture Shrine Office, "[http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=463 Kojino Shrine]" (accessed Feb. 28, 2010).. Because it was often damaged by flooding of the Yasu River, only one volume titled ''Kojino Jinja no Engi-gaki'' remains, while treasures and old records were lost. There was also a shrine temple called {{jalink|Wakaichioji Temple (Moriyama)||Wakaichioji Temple}}, and present-day {{jalink|Renko-ji (Moriyama)||Renko-ji}} is said to continue its line. ''{{jalink|Omi Yochi Shiryaku|Q85883966}}'' records a {{jalink|Yakushi Hall|Q11622438}} near the shrine with a Buddhist image said to be made by Dengyo Daishi ({{jalink|Saicho|Q449943}}), and this is inferred to have housed the shrine's {{jalink|honji-butsu}}. Later it received a Senior First Rank {{jalink|Sogen Senji}} from the {{jalink|Yoshida family|Q11413722}} of the {{jalink|Jingi Kanryo}}, and still preserves a sacred mirror inscribed "Senior First Rank Wakaichi Daigongen". Its former rank was {{jalink|village shrine|Q134917284}}. === Festivals === * Annual festival - May 5 === Precinct shrine === * Kojino Wakamiya === Kojino Shrine (Nakanomae) === {{神社 |名称 = Kojino Shrine |画像 = [[File:Kojino-jinja (Nakanomae) 202202-1.jpg|270px]]
Torii |所在地 = {{jalink|Shiga Prefecture|Q131358}}{{jalink|Moriyama|Q857225}} Sumoto-cho, Nakanomae 2322 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|05|42.0|N|135|57|56.7|E|scale:25000}} |祭神 = {{jalink|Ōanamuji-no-Mikoto}}
{{jalink|Amatsuhikone-no-Mikoto}}
{{jalink|Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} |社格 = {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} candidate shrine / former {{jalink|village shrine|Q134917284}} |創建 = tradition: {{jalink|Taiho|Q423370}} 3 (703) |本殿 = {{jalink|nagare-zukuri|Q210684|one-bay nagare-zukuri}} |別名 = Ōmagari no Kojino Shrine |札所等 = |例祭 = {{jalink|May 5|Q2550}} |神事 = Naming ritual |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Shiga|label=Kojino Shrine|coordinates= {{coord2|35|05|42.0|N|135|57|56.7|E|}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|05|42.0|N|135|57|56.7|E}}}} }} Located in Nakanomae, Sumoto-cho. From its location it is called "Nakanomae no Kojino Shrine" and "Ōmagari no Kojino Shrine" (Ōmagari was the old local area name adjacent to Nakanomae). === Enshrined deities === It enshrines {{jalink|Ōkuninushi|Q276944|Ōanamuji-no-Mikoto}}, {{jalink|Amatsuhikone|Q10940228|Amatsuhikone-no-Mikoto}}, and {{jalink|Susanoo|Q272993|Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}. === Origin === Tradition says it was founded by {{jalink|Nakatomi Tamee}} in {{jalink|703|Q23500}} ({{jalink|Taiho (Japan)|Q1346269|Taiho}} 3). Though shrine buildings were lost in warfare during the {{jalink|Eiroku|Q1143341}} era (1558-1570), they were restored by {{jalink|1791|Q7784}} ({{jalink|Kansei|Q1195131}} 3). Nearby {{jalink|Gyokurin-ji (Moriyama)||Gyokurin-ji}} was formerly its shrine temple. Its former rank was village shrine. === Festivals === * Annual festival - May 5 === Precinct shrines === * Inari Shrine * Tenmangu * Ōshiro Shrine === Cultural property === * Bronze sutra cylinder - height 25.3 {{jalink|cm|Q174728}}, diameter 11.2 cm. A {{jalink|bronze|Q753}} {{jalink|sutra container|Q11606503}} from the {{jalink|Muromachi period|Q334845}}. Designated as Moriyama City cultural property (craft) on August 26, {{jalink|1974|Q2478}} (Showa 49). === Notes === {{reflist}} === References === * ''Shikinaisha Chosa Hokoku'', Vol.12 Tōsandō 1, {{jalink|Kogakkan University||Kogakkan University Press}}, 1981 === External links === *[http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=463 Kojino Shrine] (Kaihatsu) (Shiga Prefecture Shrine Office) *[http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=464 Kojino Shrine] (Nakanomae) (Shiga Prefecture Shrine Office) {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:こしのしんしや}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Buddhism-stub}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{pref-stub|pref=滋賀県}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kojino Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kojino Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Kojino Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Disputed Shikinaisha or Shikigeisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ōmi Province]] [[Category:滋賀県の神社]] [[Category:近江国の式内社]] [[Category:滋賀県の旧村社]] [[Category:守山市の歴史]] [[Category:守山市の建築物]] [[Category:熊野信仰]] [[Category:need translation]]

''{{jalink|Omi Yochi Shiryaku|Q85883966}}'' records a {{jalink|Yakushi Hall|Q11622438}} near the shrine with a Buddhist image said to be made by Dengyo Daishi ({{jalink|Saicho|Q449943}}), and this is inferred to have housed the shrine's {{jalink|honji-butsu}}. Later it received a Senior First Rank {{jalink|Sogen Senji}} from the {{jalink|Yoshida family|Q11413722}} of the {{jalink|Jingi Kanryo}}, and still preserves a sacred mirror inscribed "Senior First Rank Wakaichi Daigongen". Its former rank was {{jalink|village shrine|Q134917284}}. === Festivals === * Annual festival - May 5 === Precinct shrine === * Kojino Wakamiya === Kojino Shrine (Nakanomae) === {{神社 |名称 = Kojino Shrine |画像 = [[File:Kojino-jinja (Nakanomae) 202202-1.jpg|270px]]
Torii |所在地 = {{jalink|Shiga Prefecture|Q131358}}{{jalink|Moriyama|Q857225}} Sumoto-cho, Nakanomae 2322 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|05|42.0|N|135|57|56.7|E|scale:25000}} |祭神 = {{jalink|Ōanamuji-no-Mikoto}}
{{jalink|Amatsuhikone-no-Mikoto}}
{{jalink|Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} |社格 = {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} candidate shrine / former {{jalink|village shrine|Q134917284}} |創建 = tradition: {{jalink|Taiho|Q423370}} 3 (703) |本殿 = {{jalink|nagare-zukuri|Q210684|one-bay nagare-zukuri}} |別名 = Ōmagari no Kojino Shrine |札所等 = |例祭 = {{jalink|May 5|Q2550}} |神事 = Naming ritual |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Shiga|label=Kojino Shrine|coordinates= {{coord2|35|05|42.0|N|135|57|56.7|E|}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|05|42.0|N|135|57|56.7|E}}}} }} Located in Nakanomae, Sumoto-cho. From its location it is called "Nakanomae no Kojino Shrine" and "Ōmagari no Kojino Shrine" (Ōmagari was the old local area name adjacent to Nakanomae). === Enshrined deities === It enshrines {{jalink|Ōkuninushi|Q276944|Ōanamuji-no-Mikoto}}, {{jalink|Amatsuhikone|Q10940228|Amatsuhikone-no-Mikoto}}, and {{jalink|Susanoo|Q272993|Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}. === Origin === Tradition says it was founded by {{jalink|Nakatomi Tamee}} in {{jalink|703|Q23500}} ({{jalink|Taiho (Japan)|Q1346269|Taiho}} 3). Though shrine buildings were lost in warfare during the {{jalink|Eiroku|Q1143341}} era (1558-1570), they were restored by {{jalink|1791|Q7784}} ({{jalink|Kansei|Q1195131}} 3). Nearby {{jalink|Gyokurin-ji (Moriyama)||Gyokurin-ji}} was formerly its shrine temple. Its former rank was village shrine. === Festivals === * Annual festival - May 5 === Precinct shrines === * Inari Shrine * Tenmangu * Ōshiro Shrine === Cultural property === * Bronze sutra cylinder - height 25.3 {{jalink|cm|Q174728}}, diameter 11.2 cm. A {{jalink|bronze|Q753}} {{jalink|sutra container|Q11606503}} from the {{jalink|Muromachi period|Q334845}}. Designated as Moriyama City cultural property (craft) on August 26, {{jalink|1974|Q2478}} (Showa 49). === Notes === {{reflist}} === References === * ''Shikinaisha Chosa Hokoku'', Vol.12 Tōsandō 1, {{jalink|Kogakkan University||Kogakkan University Press}}, 1981 === External links === *[http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=463 Kojino Shrine] (Kaihatsu) (Shiga Prefecture Shrine Office) *[http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=464 Kojino Shrine] (Nakanomae) (Shiga Prefecture Shrine Office) {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:こしのしんしや}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Buddhism-stub}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{pref-stub|pref=滋賀県}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kojino Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kojino Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Kojino Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Disputed Shikinaisha or Shikigeisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ōmi Province]] [[Category:滋賀県の神社]] [[Category:近江国の式内社]] [[Category:滋賀県の旧村社]] [[Category:守山市の歴史]] [[Category:守山市の建築物]] [[Category:熊野信仰]] [[Category:need translation]]

Kamisha|lt=Kojino Shrine Kamisha|WD=Q135193515}} ** P1352: {'amount': '+1', 'unit': '1'} ** P3831: {{ill|Q135022904|lt=Q135022904|WD=Q135022904}} * {{ill|Q135193516|lt=Q135193516|WD=Q135193516}}Kokugakuin University Shrine database ** P1352: {'amount': '+2', 'unit': '1'} ** P3831: {{ill|Q135022904|lt=Q135022904|WD=Q135022904}} == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '滋賀県守山市洲本町字開発1607', 'language': 'ja'} * {'text': '滋賀県守山市洲本町開発／中の前', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:己爾乃神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11479874}} == Japanese content == '''Kojino Shrine''' (こじのじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} in Sumoto-cho, {{jalink|Moriyama|Q857225}}, {{jalink|Shiga Prefecture|Q131358}}. In the ''{{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyocho|Q11064932}}'', under {{jalink|Yasu District|Q8049933|Yasu District of Omi Province}}, it is listed as "Kojino Shrine, two seats," and is a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}}In the ''Jinmyocho'', the character for "ko" is written with a graph formed by "small" under "person." The Kujo-family annotated text reads "Kojino" as "Konino.". Two shrines with the same name exist about 500 {{jalink|meters|Q11573}} apart. Before land reclamation, the south branch of the {{jalink|Yasu River|Q8049935}} flowing from the Yasu River to {{jalink|Lake Biwa|Q200239}} ran between them, and both stood on the left-bank levee, so they are thought to originate in river-related worship''Shikinaisha Chosa Hokoku''.. Both preserve traditions of being the Shikinaisha, but it is geographically and historically impossible to determine one as the sole original shrine; rather, it has been pointed out that the Engishiki "Kojino Shrine" may have consisted of both shrines, which may once have had close ritual ties. === Kojino Shrine (Kaihatsu) === {{神社 |名称 = Kojino Shrine |画像 = [[File:己爾乃神社 - panoramio.jpg|270px]]
Torii |所在地 = {{jalink|Shiga Prefecture|Q131358}}{{jalink|Moriyama|Q857225}} Sumoto-cho, Kaihatsu 1607 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|05|37.4|N|135|58|12.7|E|scale:25000}} |祭神 = {{jalink|Izanagi-no-Mikoto}}
{{jalink|Izanami-no-Mikoto}} |社格 = {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} candidate shrine / former {{jalink|village shrine|Q134917284}} |創建 = tradition: {{jalink|Tencho|Q1373138}} 6 (829) |本殿 = {{jalink|nagare-zukuri|Q210684|one-bay nagare-zukuri}} |別名 = Kaihatsu no Kojino Shrine |札所等 = |例祭 = {{jalink|May 5|Q2550}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Shiga|label=Kojino Shrine|coordinates= {{coord2|35|05|37.4|N|135|58|12.7|E|}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|05|37.4|N|135|58|12.7|E}}}} }} It is located in Kaihatsu, Sumoto-cho. From its location it is called "Kaihatsu no Kojino Shrine," but because a bridge called "Retsukeizu-bashi" once crossed the former south branch of the Yasu River nearby before reclamation, it was also called "Retsukeizu no Kojino Shrine". === Enshrined deities === It enshrines {{jalink|Izanagi|Q813858|Izanagi-no-Mikoto}} and {{jalink|Izanami|Q682306|Izanami-no-Mikoto}} as principal deities, with {{jalink|Ame-no-Koyane|Q2564508|Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto}} and {{jalink|Ikatsuchi no Omi no Mikoto}} as associated deities. Formerly the two associated deities were the principal deities under the names '''{{jalink|Wakaichioji|Q11616186}}''' (for Ame-no-Koyane) and '''{{jalink|Wakaichi Gongen}}''' (for Ikatsuchi-no-Omi), but on September 22, {{jalink|1881|Q7808}} (Meiji 14), they were changed to the current arrangement. === Origin === Shrine tradition says it was founded in {{jalink|829|Q30357}} ({{jalink|Tencho|Q1373138}} 6) by Iga Atsumu. Atsumu, also called "Taifu Atsumu," is said to be a descendant of the Iga clan, whose ancestor was Iga no Suketoyo. Since the Iga clan was related to the {{jalink|Nakatomi clan|Q1198447}}''{{jalink|Shinsen Shojiroku|Q1045362}}'', Sakyo Shinbetsu-jō., it is said they enshrined Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto and Ikatsuchi-no-Omi-no-Mikoto, ancestral deities of the Nakatomi clanShiga Prefecture Shrine Office, "[http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=463 Kojino Shrine]" (accessed Feb. 28, 2010).. Because it was often damaged by flooding of the Yasu River, only one volume titled ''Kojino Jinja no Engi-gaki'' remains, while treasures and old records were lost. There was also a shrine temple called {{jalink|Wakaichioji Temple (Moriyama)||Wakaichioji Temple}}, and present-day {{jalink|Renko-ji (Moriyama)||Renko-ji}} is said to continue its line. ''{{jalink|Omi Yochi Shiryaku|Q85883966}}'' records a {{jalink|Yakushi Hall|Q11622438}} near the shrine with a Buddhist image said to be made by Dengyo Daishi ({{jalink|Saicho|Q449943}}), and this is inferred to have housed the shrine's {{jalink|honji-butsu}}. Later it received a Senior First Rank {{jalink|Sogen Senji}} from the {{jalink|Yoshida family|Q11413722}} of the {{jalink|Jingi Kanryo}}, and still preserves a sacred mirror inscribed "Senior First Rank Wakaichi Daigongen". Its former rank was {{jalink|village shrine|Q134917284}}. === Festivals === * Annual festival - May 5 === Precinct shrine === * Kojino Wakamiya === Kojino Shrine (Nakanomae) === {{神社 |名称 = Kojino Shrine |画像 = [[File:Kojino-jinja (Nakanomae) 202202-1.jpg|270px]]
Torii |所在地 = {{jalink|Shiga Prefecture|Q131358}}{{jalink|Moriyama|Q857225}} Sumoto-cho, Nakanomae 2322 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|05|42.0|N|135|57|56.7|E|scale:25000}} |祭神 = {{jalink|Ōanamuji-no-Mikoto}}
{{jalink|Amatsuhikone-no-Mikoto}}
{{jalink|Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} |社格 = {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} candidate shrine / former {{jalink|village shrine|Q134917284}} |創建 = tradition: {{jalink|Taiho|Q423370}} 3 (703) |本殿 = {{jalink|nagare-zukuri|Q210684|one-bay nagare-zukuri}} |別名 = Ōmagari no Kojino Shrine |札所等 = |例祭 = {{jalink|May 5|Q2550}} |神事 = Naming ritual |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Shiga|label=Kojino Shrine|coordinates= {{coord2|35|05|42.0|N|135|57|56.7|E|}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|05|42.0|N|135|57|56.7|E}}}} }} Located in Nakanomae, Sumoto-cho. From its location it is called "Nakanomae no Kojino Shrine" and "Ōmagari no Kojino Shrine" (Ōmagari was the old local area name adjacent to Nakanomae). === Enshrined deities === It enshrines {{jalink|Ōkuninushi|Q276944|Ōanamuji-no-Mikoto}}, {{jalink|Amatsuhikone|Q10940228|Amatsuhikone-no-Mikoto}}, and {{jalink|Susanoo|Q272993|Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}. === Origin === Tradition says it was founded by {{jalink|Nakatomi Tamee}} in {{jalink|703|Q23500}} ({{jalink|Taiho (Japan)|Q1346269|Taiho}} 3). Though shrine buildings were lost in warfare during the {{jalink|Eiroku|Q1143341}} era (1558-1570), they were restored by {{jalink|1791|Q7784}} ({{jalink|Kansei|Q1195131}} 3). Nearby {{jalink|Gyokurin-ji (Moriyama)||Gyokurin-ji}} was formerly its shrine temple. Its former rank was village shrine. === Festivals === * Annual festival - May 5 === Precinct shrines === * Inari Shrine * Tenmangu * Ōshiro Shrine === Cultural property === * Bronze sutra cylinder - height 25.3 {{jalink|cm|Q174728}}, diameter 11.2 cm. A {{jalink|bronze|Q753}} {{jalink|sutra container|Q11606503}} from the {{jalink|Muromachi period|Q334845}}. Designated as Moriyama City cultural property (craft) on August 26, {{jalink|1974|Q2478}} (Showa 49). === Notes === {{reflist}} === References === * ''Shikinaisha Chosa Hokoku'', Vol.12 Tōsandō 1, {{jalink|Kogakkan University||Kogakkan University Press}}, 1981 === External links === *[http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=463 Kojino Shrine] (Kaihatsu) (Shiga Prefecture Shrine Office) *[http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=464 Kojino Shrine] (Nakanomae) (Shiga Prefecture Shrine Office) {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:こしのしんしや}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Buddhism-stub}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{pref-stub|pref=滋賀県}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kojino Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kojino Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Kojino Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Disputed Shikinaisha or Shikigeisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ōmi Province]] [[Category:滋賀県の神社]] [[Category:近江国の式内社]] [[Category:滋賀県の旧村社]] [[Category:守山市の歴史]] [[Category:守山市の建築物]] [[Category:熊野信仰]] [[Category:need translation]]

{{ill|Q135193516|lt=Q135193516|WD=Q135193516}}Kokugakuin University Shrine database ** P1352: {'amount': '+2', 'unit': '1'} ** P3831: {{ill|Q135022904|lt=Q135022904|WD=Q135022904}} == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '滋賀県守山市洲本町字開発1607', 'language': 'ja'} * {'text': '滋賀県守山市洲本町開発／中の前', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:己爾乃神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11479874}} == Japanese content == '''Kojino Shrine''' (こじのじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} in Sumoto-cho, {{jalink|Moriyama|Q857225}}, {{jalink|Shiga Prefecture|Q131358}}. In the ''{{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyocho|Q11064932}}'', under {{jalink|Yasu District|Q8049933|Yasu District of Omi Province}}, it is listed as "Kojino Shrine, two seats," and is a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}}In the ''Jinmyocho'', the character for "ko" is written with a graph formed by "small" under "person." The Kujo-family annotated text reads "Kojino" as "Konino.". Two shrines with the same name exist about 500 {{jalink|meters|Q11573}} apart. Before land reclamation, the south branch of the {{jalink|Yasu River|Q8049935}} flowing from the Yasu River to {{jalink|Lake Biwa|Q200239}} ran between them, and both stood on the left-bank levee, so they are thought to originate in river-related worship''Shikinaisha Chosa Hokoku''.. Both preserve traditions of being the Shikinaisha, but it is geographically and historically impossible to determine one as the sole original shrine; rather, it has been pointed out that the Engishiki "Kojino Shrine" may have consisted of both shrines, which may once have had close ritual ties. === Kojino Shrine (Kaihatsu) === {{神社 |名称 = Kojino Shrine |画像 = [[File:己爾乃神社 - panoramio.jpg|270px]]
Torii |所在地 = {{jalink|Shiga Prefecture|Q131358}}{{jalink|Moriyama|Q857225}} Sumoto-cho, Kaihatsu 1607 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|05|37.4|N|135|58|12.7|E|scale:25000}} |祭神 = {{jalink|Izanagi-no-Mikoto}}
{{jalink|Izanami-no-Mikoto}} |社格 = {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} candidate shrine / former {{jalink|village shrine|Q134917284}} |創建 = tradition: {{jalink|Tencho|Q1373138}} 6 (829) |本殿 = {{jalink|nagare-zukuri|Q210684|one-bay nagare-zukuri}} |別名 = Kaihatsu no Kojino Shrine |札所等 = |例祭 = {{jalink|May 5|Q2550}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Shiga|label=Kojino Shrine|coordinates= {{coord2|35|05|37.4|N|135|58|12.7|E|}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|05|37.4|N|135|58|12.7|E}}}} }} It is located in Kaihatsu, Sumoto-cho. From its location it is called "Kaihatsu no Kojino Shrine," but because a bridge called "Retsukeizu-bashi" once crossed the former south branch of the Yasu River nearby before reclamation, it was also called "Retsukeizu no Kojino Shrine". === Enshrined deities === It enshrines {{jalink|Izanagi|Q813858|Izanagi-no-Mikoto}} and {{jalink|Izanami|Q682306|Izanami-no-Mikoto}} as principal deities, with {{jalink|Ame-no-Koyane|Q2564508|Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto}} and {{jalink|Ikatsuchi no Omi no Mikoto}} as associated deities. Formerly the two associated deities were the principal deities under the names '''{{jalink|Wakaichioji|Q11616186}}''' (for Ame-no-Koyane) and '''{{jalink|Wakaichi Gongen}}''' (for Ikatsuchi-no-Omi), but on September 22, {{jalink|1881|Q7808}} (Meiji 14), they were changed to the current arrangement. === Origin === Shrine tradition says it was founded in {{jalink|829|Q30357}} ({{jalink|Tencho|Q1373138}} 6) by Iga Atsumu. Atsumu, also called "Taifu Atsumu," is said to be a descendant of the Iga clan, whose ancestor was Iga no Suketoyo. Since the Iga clan was related to the {{jalink|Nakatomi clan|Q1198447}}''{{jalink|Shinsen Shojiroku|Q1045362}}'', Sakyo Shinbetsu-jō., it is said they enshrined Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto and Ikatsuchi-no-Omi-no-Mikoto, ancestral deities of the Nakatomi clanShiga Prefecture Shrine Office, "[http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=463 Kojino Shrine]" (accessed Feb. 28, 2010).. Because it was often damaged by flooding of the Yasu River, only one volume titled ''Kojino Jinja no Engi-gaki'' remains, while treasures and old records were lost. There was also a shrine temple called {{jalink|Wakaichioji Temple (Moriyama)||Wakaichioji Temple}}, and present-day {{jalink|Renko-ji (Moriyama)||Renko-ji}} is said to continue its line. ''{{jalink|Omi Yochi Shiryaku|Q85883966}}'' records a {{jalink|Yakushi Hall|Q11622438}} near the shrine with a Buddhist image said to be made by Dengyo Daishi ({{jalink|Saicho|Q449943}}), and this is inferred to have housed the shrine's {{jalink|honji-butsu}}. Later it received a Senior First Rank {{jalink|Sogen Senji}} from the {{jalink|Yoshida family|Q11413722}} of the {{jalink|Jingi Kanryo}}, and still preserves a sacred mirror inscribed "Senior First Rank Wakaichi Daigongen". Its former rank was {{jalink|village shrine|Q134917284}}. === Festivals === * Annual festival - May 5 === Precinct shrine === * Kojino Wakamiya === Kojino Shrine (Nakanomae) === {{神社 |名称 = Kojino Shrine |画像 = [[File:Kojino-jinja (Nakanomae) 202202-1.jpg|270px]]
Torii |所在地 = {{jalink|Shiga Prefecture|Q131358}}{{jalink|Moriyama|Q857225}} Sumoto-cho, Nakanomae 2322 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|05|42.0|N|135|57|56.7|E|scale:25000}} |祭神 = {{jalink|Ōanamuji-no-Mikoto}}
{{jalink|Amatsuhikone-no-Mikoto}}
{{jalink|Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} |社格 = {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} candidate shrine / former {{jalink|village shrine|Q134917284}} |創建 = tradition: {{jalink|Taiho|Q423370}} 3 (703) |本殿 = {{jalink|nagare-zukuri|Q210684|one-bay nagare-zukuri}} |別名 = Ōmagari no Kojino Shrine |札所等 = |例祭 = {{jalink|May 5|Q2550}} |神事 = Naming ritual |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Shiga|label=Kojino Shrine|coordinates= {{coord2|35|05|42.0|N|135|57|56.7|E|}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|05|42.0|N|135|57|56.7|E}}}} }} Located in Nakanomae, Sumoto-cho. From its location it is called "Nakanomae no Kojino Shrine" and "Ōmagari no Kojino Shrine" (Ōmagari was the old local area name adjacent to Nakanomae). === Enshrined deities === It enshrines {{jalink|Ōkuninushi|Q276944|Ōanamuji-no-Mikoto}}, {{jalink|Amatsuhikone|Q10940228|Amatsuhikone-no-Mikoto}}, and {{jalink|Susanoo|Q272993|Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}. === Origin === Tradition says it was founded by {{jalink|Nakatomi Tamee}} in {{jalink|703|Q23500}} ({{jalink|Taiho (Japan)|Q1346269|Taiho}} 3). Though shrine buildings were lost in warfare during the {{jalink|Eiroku|Q1143341}} era (1558-1570), they were restored by {{jalink|1791|Q7784}} ({{jalink|Kansei|Q1195131}} 3). Nearby {{jalink|Gyokurin-ji (Moriyama)||Gyokurin-ji}} was formerly its shrine temple. Its former rank was village shrine. === Festivals === * Annual festival - May 5 === Precinct shrines === * Inari Shrine * Tenmangu * Ōshiro Shrine === Cultural property === * Bronze sutra cylinder - height 25.3 {{jalink|cm|Q174728}}, diameter 11.2 cm. A {{jalink|bronze|Q753}} {{jalink|sutra container|Q11606503}} from the {{jalink|Muromachi period|Q334845}}. Designated as Moriyama City cultural property (craft) on August 26, {{jalink|1974|Q2478}} (Showa 49). === Notes === {{reflist}} === References === * ''Shikinaisha Chosa Hokoku'', Vol.12 Tōsandō 1, {{jalink|Kogakkan University||Kogakkan University Press}}, 1981 === External links === *[http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=463 Kojino Shrine] (Kaihatsu) (Shiga Prefecture Shrine Office) *[http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=464 Kojino Shrine] (Nakanomae) (Shiga Prefecture Shrine Office) {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:こしのしんしや}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Buddhism-stub}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{pref-stub|pref=滋賀県}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kojino Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kojino Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Kojino Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Disputed Shikinaisha or Shikigeisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ōmi Province]] [[Category:滋賀県の神社]] [[Category:近江国の式内社]] [[Category:滋賀県の旧村社]] [[Category:守山市の歴史]] [[Category:守山市の建築物]] [[Category:熊野信仰]] [[Category:need translation]]

(こじのじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} in Sumoto-cho, {{jalink|Moriyama|Q857225}}, {{jalink|Shiga Prefecture|Q131358}}. In the ''{{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyocho|Q11064932}}'', under {{jalink|Yasu District|Q8049933|Yasu District of Omi Province}}, it is listed as "Kojino Shrine, two seats," and is a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}}In the ''Jinmyocho'', the character for "ko" is written with a graph formed by "small" under "person." The Kujo-family annotated text reads "Kojino" as "Konino.". Two shrines with the same name exist about 500 {{jalink|meters|Q11573}} apart. Before land reclamation, the south branch of the {{jalink|Yasu River|Q8049935}} flowing from the Yasu River to {{jalink|Lake Biwa|Q200239}} ran between them, and both stood on the left-bank levee, so they are thought to originate in river-related worship''Shikinaisha Chosa Hokoku''.. Both preserve traditions of being the Shikinaisha, but it is geographically and historically impossible to determine one as the sole original shrine; rather, it has been pointed out that the Engishiki "Kojino Shrine" may have consisted of both shrines, which may once have had close ritual ties. === Kojino Shrine (Kaihatsu) === {{神社 |名称 = Kojino Shrine |画像 = [[File:己爾乃神社 - panoramio.jpg|270px]]
Torii |所在地 = {{jalink|Shiga Prefecture|Q131358}}{{jalink|Moriyama|Q857225}} Sumoto-cho, Kaihatsu 1607 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|05|37.4|N|135|58|12.7|E|scale:25000}} |祭神 = {{jalink|Izanagi-no-Mikoto}}
{{jalink|Izanami-no-Mikoto}} |社格 = {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} candidate shrine / former {{jalink|village shrine|Q134917284}} |創建 = tradition: {{jalink|Tencho|Q1373138}} 6 (829) |本殿 = {{jalink|nagare-zukuri|Q210684|one-bay nagare-zukuri}} |別名 = Kaihatsu no Kojino Shrine |札所等 = |例祭 = {{jalink|May 5|Q2550}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Shiga|label=Kojino Shrine|coordinates= {{coord2|35|05|37.4|N|135|58|12.7|E|}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|05|37.4|N|135|58|12.7|E}}}} }} It is located in Kaihatsu, Sumoto-cho. From its location it is called "Kaihatsu no Kojino Shrine," but because a bridge called "Retsukeizu-bashi" once crossed the former south branch of the Yasu River nearby before reclamation, it was also called "Retsukeizu no Kojino Shrine". === Enshrined deities === It enshrines {{jalink|Izanagi|Q813858|Izanagi-no-Mikoto}} and {{jalink|Izanami|Q682306|Izanami-no-Mikoto}} as principal deities, with {{jalink|Ame-no-Koyane|Q2564508|Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto}} and {{jalink|Ikatsuchi no Omi no Mikoto}} as associated deities. Formerly the two associated deities were the principal deities under the names '''{{jalink|Wakaichioji|Q11616186}}''' (for Ame-no-Koyane) and '''{{jalink|Wakaichi Gongen}}''' (for Ikatsuchi-no-Omi), but on September 22, {{jalink|1881|Q7808}} (Meiji 14), they were changed to the current arrangement. === Origin === Shrine tradition says it was founded in {{jalink|829|Q30357}} ({{jalink|Tencho|Q1373138}} 6) by Iga Atsumu. Atsumu, also called "Taifu Atsumu," is said to be a descendant of the Iga clan, whose ancestor was Iga no Suketoyo. Since the Iga clan was related to the {{jalink|Nakatomi clan|Q1198447}}''{{jalink|Shinsen Shojiroku|Q1045362}}'', Sakyo Shinbetsu-jō., it is said they enshrined Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto and Ikatsuchi-no-Omi-no-Mikoto, ancestral deities of the Nakatomi clanShiga Prefecture Shrine Office, "[http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=463 Kojino Shrine]" (accessed Feb. 28, 2010).. Because it was often damaged by flooding of the Yasu River, only one volume titled ''Kojino Jinja no Engi-gaki'' remains, while treasures and old records were lost. There was also a shrine temple called {{jalink|Wakaichioji Temple (Moriyama)||Wakaichioji Temple}}, and present-day {{jalink|Renko-ji (Moriyama)||Renko-ji}} is said to continue its line. ''{{jalink|Omi Yochi Shiryaku|Q85883966}}'' records a {{jalink|Yakushi Hall|Q11622438}} near the shrine with a Buddhist image said to be made by Dengyo Daishi ({{jalink|Saicho|Q449943}}), and this is inferred to have housed the shrine's {{jalink|honji-butsu}}. Later it received a Senior First Rank {{jalink|Sogen Senji}} from the {{jalink|Yoshida family|Q11413722}} of the {{jalink|Jingi Kanryo}}, and still preserves a sacred mirror inscribed "Senior First Rank Wakaichi Daigongen". Its former rank was {{jalink|village shrine|Q134917284}}. === Festivals === * Annual festival - May 5 === Precinct shrine === * Kojino Wakamiya === Kojino Shrine (Nakanomae) === {{神社 |名称 = Kojino Shrine |画像 = [[File:Kojino-jinja (Nakanomae) 202202-1.jpg|270px]]
Torii |所在地 = {{jalink|Shiga Prefecture|Q131358}}{{jalink|Moriyama|Q857225}} Sumoto-cho, Nakanomae 2322 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|05|42.0|N|135|57|56.7|E|scale:25000}} |祭神 = {{jalink|Ōanamuji-no-Mikoto}}
{{jalink|Amatsuhikone-no-Mikoto}}
{{jalink|Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} |社格 = {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} candidate shrine / former {{jalink|village shrine|Q134917284}} |創建 = tradition: {{jalink|Taiho|Q423370}} 3 (703) |本殿 = {{jalink|nagare-zukuri|Q210684|one-bay nagare-zukuri}} |別名 = Ōmagari no Kojino Shrine |札所等 = |例祭 = {{jalink|May 5|Q2550}} |神事 = Naming ritual |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Shiga|label=Kojino Shrine|coordinates= {{coord2|35|05|42.0|N|135|57|56.7|E|}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|05|42.0|N|135|57|56.7|E}}}} }} Located in Nakanomae, Sumoto-cho. From its location it is called "Nakanomae no Kojino Shrine" and "Ōmagari no Kojino Shrine" (Ōmagari was the old local area name adjacent to Nakanomae). === Enshrined deities === It enshrines {{jalink|Ōkuninushi|Q276944|Ōanamuji-no-Mikoto}}, {{jalink|Amatsuhikone|Q10940228|Amatsuhikone-no-Mikoto}}, and {{jalink|Susanoo|Q272993|Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}. === Origin === Tradition says it was founded by {{jalink|Nakatomi Tamee}} in {{jalink|703|Q23500}} ({{jalink|Taiho (Japan)|Q1346269|Taiho}} 3). Though shrine buildings were lost in warfare during the {{jalink|Eiroku|Q1143341}} era (1558-1570), they were restored by {{jalink|1791|Q7784}} ({{jalink|Kansei|Q1195131}} 3). Nearby {{jalink|Gyokurin-ji (Moriyama)||Gyokurin-ji}} was formerly its shrine temple. Its former rank was village shrine. === Festivals === * Annual festival - May 5 === Precinct shrines === * Inari Shrine * Tenmangu * Ōshiro Shrine === Cultural property === * Bronze sutra cylinder - height 25.3 {{jalink|cm|Q174728}}, diameter 11.2 cm. A {{jalink|bronze|Q753}} {{jalink|sutra container|Q11606503}} from the {{jalink|Muromachi period|Q334845}}. Designated as Moriyama City cultural property (craft) on August 26, {{jalink|1974|Q2478}} (Showa 49). === Notes === {{reflist}} === References === * ''Shikinaisha Chosa Hokoku'', Vol.12 Tōsandō 1, {{jalink|Kogakkan University||Kogakkan University Press}}, 1981 === External links === *[http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=463 Kojino Shrine] (Kaihatsu) (Shiga Prefecture Shrine Office) *[http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=464 Kojino Shrine] (Nakanomae) (Shiga Prefecture Shrine Office) {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:こしのしんしや}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Buddhism-stub}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{pref-stub|pref=滋賀県}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kojino Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kojino Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Kojino Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Disputed Shikinaisha or Shikigeisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ōmi Province]] [[Category:滋賀県の神社]] [[Category:近江国の式内社]] [[Category:滋賀県の旧村社]] [[Category:守山市の歴史]] [[Category:守山市の建築物]] [[Category:熊野信仰]] [[Category:need translation]]

From its location it is called "Nakanomae no Kojino Shrine" and "Ōmagari no Kojino Shrine" (Ōmagari was the old local area name adjacent to Nakanomae). === Enshrined deities === It enshrines {{jalink|Ōkuninushi|Q276944|Ōanamuji-no-Mikoto}}, {{jalink|Amatsuhikone|Q10940228|Amatsuhikone-no-Mikoto}}, and {{jalink|Susanoo|Q272993|Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}. === Origin === Tradition says it was founded by {{jalink|Nakatomi Tamee}} in {{jalink|703|Q23500}} ({{jalink|Taiho (Japan)|Q1346269|Taiho}} 3). Though shrine buildings were lost in warfare during the {{jalink|Eiroku|Q1143341}} era (1558-1570), they were restored by {{jalink|1791|Q7784}} ({{jalink|Kansei|Q1195131}} 3). Nearby {{jalink|Gyokurin-ji (Moriyama)||Gyokurin-ji}} was formerly its shrine temple. Its former rank was village shrine. === Festivals === * Annual festival - May 5 === Precinct shrines === * Inari Shrine * Tenmangu * Ōshiro Shrine === Cultural property === * Bronze sutra cylinder - height 25.3 {{jalink|cm|Q174728}}, diameter 11.2 cm. A {{jalink|bronze|Q753}} {{jalink|sutra container|Q11606503}} from the {{jalink|Muromachi period|Q334845}}. Designated as Moriyama City cultural property (craft) on August 26, {{jalink|1974|Q2478}} (Showa 49). === Notes === {{reflist}} === References === * ''Shikinaisha Chosa Hokoku'', Vol.12 Tōsandō 1, {{jalink|Kogakkan University||Kogakkan University Press}}, 1981 === External links === *[http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=463 Kojino Shrine] (Kaihatsu) (Shiga Prefecture Shrine Office) *[http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=464 Kojino Shrine] (Nakanomae) (Shiga Prefecture Shrine Office) {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:こしのしんしや}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Buddhism-stub}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{pref-stub|pref=滋賀県}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kojino Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kojino Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Kojino Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Disputed Shikinaisha or Shikigeisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ōmi Province]] [[Category:滋賀県の神社]] [[Category:近江国の式内社]] [[Category:滋賀県の旧村社]] [[Category:守山市の歴史]] [[Category:守山市の建築物]] [[Category:熊野信仰]] [[Category:need translation]]

(P6375) == * {'text': '滋賀県守山市洲本町字開発1607', 'language': 'ja'} * {'text': '滋賀県守山市洲本町開発／中の前', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:己爾乃神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11479874}} == Japanese content == '''Kojino Shrine''' (こじのじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} in Sumoto-cho, {{jalink|Moriyama|Q857225}}, {{jalink|Shiga Prefecture|Q131358}}. In the ''{{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyocho|Q11064932}}'', under {{jalink|Yasu District|Q8049933|Yasu District of Omi Province}}, it is listed as "Kojino Shrine, two seats," and is a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}}In the ''Jinmyocho'', the character for "ko" is written with a graph formed by "small" under "person." The Kujo-family annotated text reads "Kojino" as "Konino.". Two shrines with the same name exist about 500 {{jalink|meters|Q11573}} apart. Before land reclamation, the south branch of the {{jalink|Yasu River|Q8049935}} flowing from the Yasu River to {{jalink|Lake Biwa|Q200239}} ran between them, and both stood on the left-bank levee, so they are thought to originate in river-related worship''Shikinaisha Chosa Hokoku''.. Both preserve traditions of being the Shikinaisha, but it is geographically and historically impossible to determine one as the sole original shrine; rather, it has been pointed out that the Engishiki "Kojino Shrine" may have consisted of both shrines, which may once have had close ritual ties. === Kojino Shrine (Kaihatsu) === {{神社 |名称 = Kojino Shrine |画像 = [[File:己爾乃神社 - panoramio.jpg|270px]]
Torii |所在地 = {{jalink|Shiga Prefecture|Q131358}}{{jalink|Moriyama|Q857225}} Sumoto-cho, Kaihatsu 1607 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|05|37.4|N|135|58|12.7|E|scale:25000}} |祭神 = {{jalink|Izanagi-no-Mikoto}}
{{jalink|Izanami-no-Mikoto}} |社格 = {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} candidate shrine / former {{jalink|village shrine|Q134917284}} |創建 = tradition: {{jalink|Tencho|Q1373138}} 6 (829) |本殿 = {{jalink|nagare-zukuri|Q210684|one-bay nagare-zukuri}} |別名 = Kaihatsu no Kojino Shrine |札所等 = |例祭 = {{jalink|May 5|Q2550}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Shiga|label=Kojino Shrine|coordinates= {{coord2|35|05|37.4|N|135|58|12.7|E|}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|05|37.4|N|135|58|12.7|E}}}} }} It is located in Kaihatsu, Sumoto-cho. From its location it is called "Kaihatsu no Kojino Shrine," but because a bridge called "Retsukeizu-bashi" once crossed the former south branch of the Yasu River nearby before reclamation, it was also called "Retsukeizu no Kojino Shrine". === Enshrined deities === It enshrines {{jalink|Izanagi|Q813858|Izanagi-no-Mikoto}} and {{jalink|Izanami|Q682306|Izanami-no-Mikoto}} as principal deities, with {{jalink|Ame-no-Koyane|Q2564508|Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto}} and {{jalink|Ikatsuchi no Omi no Mikoto}} as associated deities. Formerly the two associated deities were the principal deities under the names '''{{jalink|Wakaichioji|Q11616186}}''' (for Ame-no-Koyane) and '''{{jalink|Wakaichi Gongen}}''' (for Ikatsuchi-no-Omi), but on September 22, {{jalink|1881|Q7808}} (Meiji 14), they were changed to the current arrangement. === Origin === Shrine tradition says it was founded in {{jalink|829|Q30357}} ({{jalink|Tencho|Q1373138}} 6) by Iga Atsumu. Atsumu, also called "Taifu Atsumu," is said to be a descendant of the Iga clan, whose ancestor was Iga no Suketoyo. Since the Iga clan was related to the {{jalink|Nakatomi clan|Q1198447}}''{{jalink|Shinsen Shojiroku|Q1045362}}'', Sakyo Shinbetsu-jō., it is said they enshrined Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto and Ikatsuchi-no-Omi-no-Mikoto, ancestral deities of the Nakatomi clanShiga Prefecture Shrine Office, "[http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=463 Kojino Shrine]" (accessed Feb. 28, 2010).. Because it was often damaged by flooding of the Yasu River, only one volume titled ''Kojino Jinja no Engi-gaki'' remains, while treasures and old records were lost. There was also a shrine temple called {{jalink|Wakaichioji Temple (Moriyama)||Wakaichioji Temple}}, and present-day {{jalink|Renko-ji (Moriyama)||Renko-ji}} is said to continue its line. ''{{jalink|Omi Yochi Shiryaku|Q85883966}}'' records a {{jalink|Yakushi Hall|Q11622438}} near the shrine with a Buddhist image said to be made by Dengyo Daishi ({{jalink|Saicho|Q449943}}), and this is inferred to have housed the shrine's {{jalink|honji-butsu}}. Later it received a Senior First Rank {{jalink|Sogen Senji}} from the {{jalink|Yoshida family|Q11413722}} of the {{jalink|Jingi Kanryo}}, and still preserves a sacred mirror inscribed "Senior First Rank Wakaichi Daigongen". Its former rank was {{jalink|village shrine|Q134917284}}. === Festivals === * Annual festival - May 5 === Precinct shrine === * Kojino Wakamiya === Kojino Shrine (Nakanomae) === {{神社 |名称 = Kojino Shrine |画像 = [[File:Kojino-jinja (Nakanomae) 202202-1.jpg|270px]]
Torii |所在地 = {{jalink|Shiga Prefecture|Q131358}}{{jalink|Moriyama|Q857225}} Sumoto-cho, Nakanomae 2322 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|05|42.0|N|135|57|56.7|E|scale:25000}} |祭神 = {{jalink|Ōanamuji-no-Mikoto}}
{{jalink|Amatsuhikone-no-Mikoto}}
{{jalink|Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} |社格 = {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} candidate shrine / former {{jalink|village shrine|Q134917284}} |創建 = tradition: {{jalink|Taiho|Q423370}} 3 (703) |本殿 = {{jalink|nagare-zukuri|Q210684|one-bay nagare-zukuri}} |別名 = Ōmagari no Kojino Shrine |札所等 = |例祭 = {{jalink|May 5|Q2550}} |神事 = Naming ritual |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Shiga|label=Kojino Shrine|coordinates= {{coord2|35|05|42.0|N|135|57|56.7|E|}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|05|42.0|N|135|57|56.7|E}}}} }} Located in Nakanomae, Sumoto-cho. From its location it is called "Nakanomae no Kojino Shrine" and "Ōmagari no Kojino Shrine" (Ōmagari was the old local area name adjacent to Nakanomae). === Enshrined deities === It enshrines {{jalink|Ōkuninushi|Q276944|Ōanamuji-no-Mikoto}}, {{jalink|Amatsuhikone|Q10940228|Amatsuhikone-no-Mikoto}}, and {{jalink|Susanoo|Q272993|Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}. === Origin === Tradition says it was founded by {{jalink|Nakatomi Tamee}} in {{jalink|703|Q23500}} ({{jalink|Taiho (Japan)|Q1346269|Taiho}} 3). Though shrine buildings were lost in warfare during the {{jalink|Eiroku|Q1143341}} era (1558-1570), they were restored by {{jalink|1791|Q7784}} ({{jalink|Kansei|Q1195131}} 3). Nearby {{jalink|Gyokurin-ji (Moriyama)||Gyokurin-ji}} was formerly its shrine temple. Its former rank was village shrine. === Festivals === * Annual festival - May 5 === Precinct shrines === * Inari Shrine * Tenmangu * Ōshiro Shrine === Cultural property === * Bronze sutra cylinder - height 25.3 {{jalink|cm|Q174728}}, diameter 11.2 cm. A {{jalink|bronze|Q753}} {{jalink|sutra container|Q11606503}} from the {{jalink|Muromachi period|Q334845}}. Designated as Moriyama City cultural property (craft) on August 26, {{jalink|1974|Q2478}} (Showa 49). === Notes === {{reflist}} === References === * ''Shikinaisha Chosa Hokoku'', Vol.12 Tōsandō 1, {{jalink|Kogakkan University||Kogakkan University Press}}, 1981 === External links === *[http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=463 Kojino Shrine] (Kaihatsu) (Shiga Prefecture Shrine Office) *[http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=464 Kojino Shrine] (Nakanomae) (Shiga Prefecture Shrine Office) {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:こしのしんしや}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Buddhism-stub}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{pref-stub|pref=滋賀県}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kojino Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kojino Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Kojino Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Disputed Shikinaisha or Shikigeisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ōmi Province]] [[Category:滋賀県の神社]] [[Category:近江国の式内社]] [[Category:滋賀県の旧村社]] [[Category:守山市の歴史]] [[Category:守山市の建築物]] [[Category:熊野信仰]]

(P6375) == * {'text': '滋賀県守山市洲本町字開発1607', 'language': 'ja'} * {'text': '滋賀県守山市洲本町開発／中の前', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:己爾乃神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11479874}} == Japanese content == '''Kojino Shrine''' (こじのじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} in Sumoto-cho, {{jalink|Moriyama|Q857225}}, {{jalink|Shiga Prefecture|Q131358}}. In the ''{{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyocho|Q11064932}}'', under {{jalink|Yasu District|Q8049933|Yasu District of Omi Province}}, it is listed as "Kojino Shrine, two seats," and is a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}}In the ''Jinmyocho'', the character for "ko" is written with a graph formed by "small" under "person." The Kujo-family annotated text reads "Kojino" as "Konino.". Two shrines with the same name exist about 500 {{jalink|meters|Q11573}} apart. Before land reclamation, the south branch of the {{jalink|Yasu River|Q8049935}} flowing from the Yasu River to {{jalink|Lake Biwa|Q200239}} ran between them, and both stood on the left-bank levee, so they are thought to originate in river-related worship''Shikinaisha Chosa Hokoku''.. Both preserve traditions of being the Shikinaisha, but it is geographically and historically impossible to determine one as the sole original shrine; rather, it has been pointed out that the Engishiki "Kojino Shrine" may have consisted of both shrines, which may once have had close ritual ties. === Kojino Shrine (Kaihatsu) === {{神社 |名称 = Kojino Shrine |画像 = [[File:己爾乃神社 - panoramio.jpg|270px]]
Torii |所在地 = {{jalink|Shiga Prefecture|Q131358}}{{jalink|Moriyama|Q857225}} Sumoto-cho, Kaihatsu 1607 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|05|37.4|N|135|58|12.7|E|scale:25000}} |祭神 = {{jalink|Izanagi-no-Mikoto}}
{{jalink|Izanami-no-Mikoto}} |社格 = {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} candidate shrine / former {{jalink|village shrine|Q134917284}} |創建 = tradition: {{jalink|Tencho|Q1373138}} 6 (829) |本殿 = {{jalink|nagare-zukuri|Q210684|one-bay nagare-zukuri}} |別名 = Kaihatsu no Kojino Shrine |札所等 = |例祭 = {{jalink|May 5|Q2550}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Shiga|label=Kojino Shrine|coordinates= {{coord2|35|05|37.4|N|135|58|12.7|E|}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|05|37.4|N|135|58|12.7|E}}}} }} It is located in Kaihatsu, Sumoto-cho. From its location it is called "Kaihatsu no Kojino Shrine," but because a bridge called "Retsukeizu-bashi" once crossed the former south branch of the Yasu River nearby before reclamation, it was also called "Retsukeizu no Kojino Shrine". === Enshrined deities === It enshrines {{jalink|Izanagi|Q813858|Izanagi-no-Mikoto}} and {{jalink|Izanami|Q682306|Izanami-no-Mikoto}} as principal deities, with {{jalink|Ame-no-Koyane|Q2564508|Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto}} and {{jalink|Ikatsuchi no Omi no Mikoto}} as associated deities. Formerly the two associated deities were the principal deities under the names '''{{jalink|Wakaichioji|Q11616186}}''' (for Ame-no-Koyane) and '''{{jalink|Wakaichi Gongen}}''' (for Ikatsuchi-no-Omi), but on September 22, {{jalink|1881|Q7808}} (Meiji 14), they were changed to the current arrangement. === Origin === Shrine tradition says it was founded in {{jalink|829|Q30357}} ({{jalink|Tencho|Q1373138}} 6) by Iga Atsumu. Atsumu, also called "Taifu Atsumu," is said to be a descendant of the Iga clan, whose ancestor was Iga no Suketoyo. Since the Iga clan was related to the {{jalink|Nakatomi clan|Q1198447}}''{{jalink|Shinsen Shojiroku|Q1045362}}'', Sakyo Shinbetsu-jō., it is said they enshrined Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto and Ikatsuchi-no-Omi-no-Mikoto, ancestral deities of the Nakatomi clanShiga Prefecture Shrine Office, "[http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=463 Kojino Shrine]" (accessed Feb. 28, 2010).. Because it was often damaged by flooding of the Yasu River, only one volume titled ''Kojino Jinja no Engi-gaki'' remains, while treasures and old records were lost. There was also a shrine temple called {{jalink|Wakaichioji Temple (Moriyama)||Wakaichioji Temple}}, and present-day {{jalink|Renko-ji (Moriyama)||Renko-ji}} is said to continue its line. ''{{jalink|Omi Yochi Shiryaku|Q85883966}}'' records a {{jalink|Yakushi Hall|Q11622438}} near the shrine with a Buddhist image said to be made by Dengyo Daishi ({{jalink|Saicho|Q449943}}), and this is inferred to have housed the shrine's {{jalink|honji-butsu}}. Later it received a Senior First Rank {{jalink|Sogen Senji}} from the {{jalink|Yoshida family|Q11413722}} of the {{jalink|Jingi Kanryo}}, and still preserves a sacred mirror inscribed "Senior First Rank Wakaichi Daigongen". Its former rank was {{jalink|village shrine|Q134917284}}. === Festivals === * Annual festival - May 5 === Precinct shrine === * Kojino Wakamiya === Kojino Shrine (Nakanomae) === {{神社 |名称 = Kojino Shrine |画像 = [[File:Kojino-jinja (Nakanomae) 202202-1.jpg|270px]]
Torii |所在地 = {{jalink|Shiga Prefecture|Q131358}}{{jalink|Moriyama|Q857225}} Sumoto-cho, Nakanomae 2322 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|05|42.0|N|135|57|56.7|E|scale:25000}} |祭神 = {{jalink|Ōanamuji-no-Mikoto}}
{{jalink|Amatsuhikone-no-Mikoto}}
{{jalink|Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} |社格 = {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} candidate shrine / former {{jalink|village shrine|Q134917284}} |創建 = tradition: {{jalink|Taiho|Q423370}} 3 (703) |本殿 = {{jalink|nagare-zukuri|Q210684|one-bay nagare-zukuri}} |別名 = Ōmagari no Kojino Shrine |札所等 = |例祭 = {{jalink|May 5|Q2550}} |神事 = Naming ritual |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Shiga|label=Kojino Shrine|coordinates= {{coord2|35|05|42.0|N|135|57|56.7|E|}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|05|42.0|N|135|57|56.7|E}}}} }} Located in Nakanomae, Sumoto-cho. From its location it is called "Nakanomae no Kojino Shrine" and "Ōmagari no Kojino Shrine" (Ōmagari was the old local area name adjacent to Nakanomae). === Enshrined deities === It enshrines {{jalink|Ōkuninushi|Q276944|Ōanamuji-no-Mikoto}}, {{jalink|Amatsuhikone|Q10940228|Amatsuhikone-no-Mikoto}}, and {{jalink|Susanoo|Q272993|Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}. === Origin === Tradition says it was founded by {{jalink|Nakatomi Tamee}} in {{jalink|703|Q23500}} ({{jalink|Taiho (Japan)|Q1346269|Taiho}} 3). Though shrine buildings were lost in warfare during the {{jalink|Eiroku|Q1143341}} era (1558-1570), they were restored by {{jalink|1791|Q7784}} ({{jalink|Kansei|Q1195131}} 3). Nearby {{jalink|Gyokurin-ji (Moriyama)||Gyokurin-ji}} was formerly its shrine temple. Its former rank was village shrine. === Festivals === * Annual festival - May 5 === Precinct shrines === * Inari Shrine * Tenmangu * Ōshiro Shrine === Cultural property === * Bronze sutra cylinder - height 25.3 {{jalink|cm|Q174728}}, diameter 11.2 cm. A {{jalink|bronze|Q753}} {{jalink|sutra container|Q11606503}} from the {{jalink|Muromachi period|Q334845}}. Designated as Moriyama City cultural property (craft) on August 26, {{jalink|1974|Q2478}} (Showa 49). === Notes === {{reflist}} === References === * ''Shikinaisha Chosa Hokoku'', Vol.12 Tōsandō 1, {{jalink|Kogakkan University||Kogakkan University Press}}, 1981 === External links === *[http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=463 Kojino Shrine] (Kaihatsu) (Shiga Prefecture Shrine Office) *[http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=464 Kojino Shrine] (Nakanomae) (Shiga Prefecture Shrine Office) {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:こしのしんしや}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Buddhism-stub}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{pref-stub|pref=滋賀県}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kojino Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kojino Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Kojino Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Disputed Shikinaisha or Shikigeisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ōmi Province]] [[Category:滋賀県の神社]] [[Category:近江国の式内社]] [[Category:滋賀県の旧村社]] [[Category:守山市の歴史]] [[Category:守山市の建築物]] [[Category:熊野信仰]][[Category:qq]]

From its location it is called "Kaihatsu no Kojino Shrine," but because a bridge called "Retsukeizu-bashi" once crossed the former south branch of the Yasu River nearby before reclamation, it was also called "Retsukeizu no Kojino Shrine". === Enshrined deities === It enshrines {{jalink|Izanagi|Q813858|Izanagi-no-Mikoto}} and {{jalink|Izanami|Q682306|Izanami-no-Mikoto}} as principal deities, with {{jalink|Ame-no-Koyane|Q2564508|Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto}} and {{jalink|Ikatsuchi no Omi no Mikoto}} as associated deities. Formerly the two associated deities were the principal deities under the names '''{{jalink|Wakaichioji|Q11616186}}''' (for Ame-no-Koyane) and '''{{jalink|Wakaichi Gongen}}''' (for Ikatsuchi-no-Omi), but on September 22, {{jalink|1881|Q7808}} (Meiji 14), they were changed to the current arrangement. === Origin === Shrine tradition says it was founded in {{jalink|829|Q30357}} ({{jalink|Tencho|Q1373138}} 6) by Iga Atsumu. Atsumu, also called "Taifu Atsumu," is said to be a descendant of the Iga clan, whose ancestor was Iga no Suketoyo. Since the Iga clan was related to the {{jalink|Nakatomi clan|Q1198447}}''{{jalink|Shinsen Shojiroku|Q1045362}}'', Sakyo Shinbetsu-jō., it is said they enshrined Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto and Ikatsuchi-no-Omi-no-Mikoto, ancestral deities of the Nakatomi clanShiga Prefecture Shrine Office, "[http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=463 Kojino Shrine]" (accessed Feb. 28, 2010).. Because it was often damaged by flooding of the Yasu River, only one volume titled ''Kojino Jinja no Engi-gaki'' remains, while treasures and old records were lost. There was also a shrine temple called {{jalink|Wakaichioji Temple (Moriyama)||Wakaichioji Temple}}, and present-day {{jalink|Renko-ji (Moriyama)||Renko-ji}} is said to continue its line. ''{{jalink|Omi Yochi Shiryaku|Q85883966}}'' records a {{jalink|Yakushi Hall|Q11622438}} near the shrine with a Buddhist image said to be made by Dengyo Daishi ({{jalink|Saicho|Q449943}}), and this is inferred to have housed the shrine's {{jalink|honji-butsu}}. Later it received a Senior First Rank {{jalink|Sogen Senji}} from the {{jalink|Yoshida family|Q11413722}} of the {{jalink|Jingi Kanryo}}, and still preserves a sacred mirror inscribed "Senior First Rank Wakaichi Daigongen". Its former rank was {{jalink|village shrine|Q134917284}}. === Festivals === * Annual festival - May 5 === Precinct shrine === * Kojino Wakamiya === Kojino Shrine (Nakanomae) === {{神社 |名称 = Kojino Shrine |画像 = [[File:Kojino-jinja (Nakanomae) 202202-1.jpg|270px]]

From its location it is called "Nakanomae no Kojino Shrine" and "Ōmagari no Kojino Shrine" (Ōmagari was the old local area name adjacent to Nakanomae). === Enshrined deities === It enshrines {{jalink|Ōkuninushi|Q276944|Ōanamuji-no-Mikoto}}, {{jalink|Amatsuhikone|Q10940228|Amatsuhikone-no-Mikoto}}, and {{jalink|Susanoo|Q272993|Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}. === Origin === Tradition says it was founded by {{jalink|Nakatomi Tamee}} in {{jalink|703|Q23500}} ({{jalink|Taiho (Japan)|Q1346269|Taiho}} 3). Though shrine buildings were lost in warfare during the {{jalink|Eiroku|Q1143341}} era (1558-1570), they were restored by {{jalink|1791|Q7784}} ({{jalink|Kansei|Q1195131}} 3). Nearby {{jalink|Gyokurin-ji (Moriyama)||Gyokurin-ji}} was formerly its shrine temple. Its former rank was village shrine. === Festivals === * Annual festival - May 5 === Precinct shrines === * Inari Shrine * Tenmangu * Ōshiro Shrine === Cultural property === * Bronze sutra cylinder - height 25.3 {{jalink|cm|Q174728}}, diameter 11.2 cm. A {{jalink|bronze|Q753}} {{jalink|sutra container|Q11606503}} from the {{jalink|Muromachi period|Q334845}}. (P6375) == * {'text': '滋賀県守山市洲本町字開発1607', 'language': 'ja'} * {'text': '滋賀県守山市洲本町開発／中の前', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:己爾乃神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11479874}} == Japanese content == '''Kojino Shrine''' (こじのじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in Sumoto-cho, {{ill|lt=Moriyama|Q857225|WD=Q857225}}, {{ill|lt=Shiga Prefecture|Q131358|WD=Q131358}}. In the ''{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyocho|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}}'', under {{ill|lt=Yasu District|Q8049933|WD=Q8049933|lt=Yasu District of Omi Province}}, it is listed as "Kojino Shrine, two seats," and is a {{ill||lt=Shikinaisha|UNKNOWN}}In the ''Jinmyocho'', the character for "ko" is written with a graph formed by "small" under "person." The Kujo-family annotated text reads "Kojino" as "Konino.". Two shrines with the same name exist about 500 {{ill|lt=meters|Q11573|WD=Q11573}} apart. Before land reclamation, the south branch of the {{ill|lt=Yasu River|Q8049935|WD=Q8049935}} flowing from the Yasu River to {{ill|lt=Lake Biwa|Q200239|WD=Q200239}} ran between them, and both stood on the left-bank levee, so they are thought to originate in river-related worship''Shikinaisha Chosa Hokoku''.. Both preserve traditions of being the Shikinaisha, but it is geographically and historically impossible to determine one as the sole original shrine; rather, it has been pointed out that the Engishiki "Kojino Shrine" may have consisted of both shrines, which may once have had close ritual ties. === Kojino Shrine (Kaihatsu) === {{神社 |名称 = Kojino Shrine |画像 = [[File:己爾乃神社 - panoramio.jpg|270px]]

(P6375) == * {'text': '滋賀県守山市洲本町字開発1607', 'language': 'ja'} * {'text': '滋賀県守山市洲本町開発／中の前', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:己爾乃神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11479874}} == Japanese content == '''Kojino Shrine''' (こじのじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in Sumoto-cho, {{ill|lt=Moriyama|Q857225|WD=Q857225}}, {{ill|lt=Shiga Prefecture|Q131358|WD=Q131358}}. In the ''{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyocho|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}}'', under {{ill|lt=Yasu District|Q8049933|WD=Q8049933|lt=Yasu District of Omi Province}}, it is listed as "Kojino Shrine, two seats," and is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|UNKNOWN}}In the ''Jinmyocho'', the character for "ko" is written with a graph formed by "small" under "person." The Kujo-family annotated text reads "Kojino" as "Konino.". Two shrines with the same name exist about 500 {{ill|lt=meters|Q11573|WD=Q11573}} apart. Before land reclamation, the south branch of the {{ill|lt=Yasu River|Q8049935|WD=Q8049935}} flowing from the Yasu River to {{ill|lt=Lake Biwa|Q200239|WD=Q200239}} ran between them, and both stood on the left-bank levee, so they are thought to originate in river-related worship''Shikinaisha Chosa Hokoku''.. Both preserve traditions of being the Shikinaisha, but it is geographically and historically impossible to determine one as the sole original shrine; rather, it has been pointed out that the Engishiki "Kojino Shrine" may have consisted of both shrines, which may once have had close ritual ties. === Kojino Shrine (Kaihatsu) === {{神社 |名称 = Kojino Shrine |画像 = [[File:己爾乃神社 - panoramio.jpg|270px]]
Torii |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Shiga Prefecture|Q131358|WD=Q131358}}{{ill|lt=Moriyama|Q857225|WD=Q857225}} Sumoto-cho, Kaihatsu 1607 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|05|37.4|N|135|58|12.7|E|scale:25000}} |祭神 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Izanagi-no-Mikoto|UNKNOWN}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Izanami-no-Mikoto|UNKNOWN}} |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|UNKNOWN}} candidate shrine / former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}} |創建 = tradition: {{ill|lt=Tencho|Q1373138|WD=Q1373138}} 6 (829) |本殿 = {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|lt=one-bay nagare-zukuri}} |別名 = Kaihatsu no Kojino Shrine |札所等 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=May 5|Q2550|WD=Q2550}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Shiga|label=Kojino Shrine|coordinates= {{coord2|35|05|37.4|N|135|58|12.7|E|}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|05|37.4|N|135|58|12.7|E}}}} }} It is located in Kaihatsu, Sumoto-cho. From its location it is called "Kaihatsu no Kojino Shrine," but because a bridge called "Retsukeizu-bashi" once crossed the former south branch of the Yasu River nearby before reclamation, it was also called "Retsukeizu no Kojino Shrine". === Enshrined deities === It enshrines {{ill|lt=Izanagi|Q813858|WD=Q813858|lt=Izanagi-no-Mikoto}} and {{ill|lt=Izanami|Q682306|WD=Q682306|lt=Izanami-no-Mikoto}} as principal deities, with {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Koyane|Q2564508|WD=Q2564508|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto}} and {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ikatsuchi no Omi no Mikoto|UNKNOWN}} as associated deities. Formerly the two associated deities were the principal deities under the names '''{{ill|lt=Wakaichioji|Q11616186|WD=Q11616186}}''' (for Ame-no-Koyane) and '''{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Wakaichi Gongen|UNKNOWN}}''' (for Ikatsuchi-no-Omi), but on September 22, {{ill|lt=1881|Q7808|WD=Q7808}} (Meiji 14), they were changed to the current arrangement. === Origin === Shrine tradition says it was founded in {{ill|lt=829|Q30357|WD=Q30357}} ({{ill|lt=Tencho|Q1373138|WD=Q1373138}} 6) by Iga Atsumu. Atsumu, also called "Taifu Atsumu," is said to be a descendant of the Iga clan, whose ancestor was Iga no Suketoyo. Since the Iga clan was related to the {{ill|lt=Nakatomi clan|Q1198447|WD=Q1198447}}''{{ill|lt=Shinsen Shojiroku|Q1045362|WD=Q1045362}}'', Sakyo Shinbetsu-jō., it is said they enshrined Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto and Ikatsuchi-no-Omi-no-Mikoto, ancestral deities of the Nakatomi clanShiga Prefecture Shrine Office, "[http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=463 Kojino Shrine]" (accessed Feb. 28, 2010).. Because it was often damaged by flooding of the Yasu River, only one volume titled ''Kojino Jinja no Engi-gaki'' remains, while treasures and old records were lost. There was also a shrine temple called {{ill|lt=Wakaichioji Temple (Moriyama)||WD=|lt=Wakaichioji Temple}}, and present-day {{ill|lt=Renko-ji (Moriyama)||WD=|lt=Renko-ji}} is said to continue its line. ''{{ill|lt=Omi Yochi Shiryaku|Q85883966|WD=Q85883966}}'' records a {{ill|lt=Yakushi Hall|Q11622438|WD=Q11622438}} near the shrine with a Buddhist image said to be made by Dengyo Daishi ({{ill|lt=Saicho|Q449943|WD=Q449943}}), and this is inferred to have housed the shrine's {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=honji-butsu|UNKNOWN}}. Later it received a Senior First Rank {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sogen Senji|UNKNOWN}} from the {{ill|lt=Yoshida family|Q11413722|WD=Q11413722}} of the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Jingi Kanryo|UNKNOWN}}, and still preserves a sacred mirror inscribed "Senior First Rank Wakaichi Daigongen". Its former rank was {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}}. === Festivals === * Annual festival - May 5 === Precinct shrine === * Kojino Wakamiya === Kojino Shrine (Nakanomae) === {{神社 |名称 = Kojino Shrine |画像 = [[File:Kojino-jinja (Nakanomae) 202202-1.jpg|270px]]
Torii |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Shiga Prefecture|Q131358|WD=Q131358}}{{ill|lt=Moriyama|Q857225|WD=Q857225}} Sumoto-cho, Nakanomae 2322 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|05|42.0|N|135|57|56.7|E|scale:25000}} |祭神 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōanamuji-no-Mikoto|UNKNOWN}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Amatsuhikone-no-Mikoto|UNKNOWN}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|UNKNOWN}} |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|UNKNOWN}} candidate shrine / former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}} |創建 = tradition: {{ill|lt=Taiho|Q423370|WD=Q423370}} 3 (703) |本殿 = {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|lt=one-bay nagare-zukuri}} |別名 = Ōmagari no Kojino Shrine |札所等 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=May 5|Q2550|WD=Q2550}} |神事 = Naming ritual |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Shiga|label=Kojino Shrine|coordinates= {{coord2|35|05|42.0|N|135|57|56.7|E|}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|05|42.0|N|135|57|56.7|E}}}} }} Located in Nakanomae, Sumoto-cho. From its location it is called "Nakanomae no Kojino Shrine" and "Ōmagari no Kojino Shrine" (Ōmagari was the old local area name adjacent to Nakanomae). === Enshrined deities === It enshrines {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōanamuji-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|lt=Amatsuhikone|Q10940228|WD=Q10940228|lt=Amatsuhikone-no-Mikoto}}, and {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}. === Origin === Tradition says it was founded by {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Nakatomi Tamee|UNKNOWN}} in {{ill|lt=703|Q23500|WD=Q23500}} ({{ill|lt=Taiho (Japan)|Q1346269|WD=Q1346269|lt=Taiho}} 3). Though shrine buildings were lost in warfare during the {{ill|lt=Eiroku|Q1143341|WD=Q1143341}} era (1558-1570), they were restored by {{ill|lt=1791|Q7784|WD=Q7784}} ({{ill|lt=Kansei|Q1195131|WD=Q1195131}} 3). Nearby {{ill|lt=Gyokurin-ji (Moriyama)||WD=|lt=Gyokurin-ji}} was formerly its shrine temple. Its former rank was village shrine. === Festivals === * Annual festival - May 5 === Precinct shrines === * Inari Shrine * Tenmangu * Ōshiro Shrine === Cultural property === * Bronze sutra cylinder - height 25.3 {{ill|lt=cm|Q174728|WD=Q174728}}, diameter 11.2 cm. A {{ill|lt=bronze|Q753|WD=Q753}} {{ill|lt=sutra container|Q11606503|WD=Q11606503}} from the {{ill|lt=Muromachi period|Q334845|WD=Q334845}}. Designated as Moriyama City cultural property (craft) on August 26, {{ill|lt=1974|Q2478|WD=Q2478}} (Showa 49). === Notes === {{reflist}} === References === * ''Shikinaisha Chosa Hokoku'', Vol.12 Tōsandō 1, {{ill|lt=Kogakkan University||WD=|lt=Kogakkan University Press}}, 1981 === External links === *[http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=463 Kojino Shrine] (Kaihatsu) (Shiga Prefecture Shrine Office) *[http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=464 Kojino Shrine] (Nakanomae) (Shiga Prefecture Shrine Office) {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:こしのしんしや}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Buddhism-stub}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{pref-stub|pref=滋賀県}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kojino Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kojino Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Kojino Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Disputed Shikinaisha or Shikigeisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ōmi Province]] [[Category:滋賀県の神社]] [[Category:近江国の式内社]] [[Category:滋賀県の旧村社]] [[Category:守山市の歴史]] [[Category:守山市の建築物]] [[Category:熊野信仰]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]
