'language': 'ja'} [[JA:己爾乃神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11479874}} == Japanese content == '''Kojino Shrine''' (こじのじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|qid=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Sumoto-cho, {{ill|lt=Moriyama|Q857225|qid=Q857225|1=Moriyama, Shiga}}, {{ill|lt=Shiga Prefecture|Q131358|qid=Q131358|1=Shiga Prefecture}}. In the ''{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyocho|Q11064932|qid=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}'', under {{ill|lt=Yasu District|Q8049933|qid=Q8049933|lt=Yasu District of Ōmi Province|1=Yasu District, Shiga}}, it is listed as "Kojino Shrine, two seats," and is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}}In the ''Jinmyocho'', the character for "ko" is written with a graph formed by "small" under "person." The Kujo-family annotated text reads "Kojino" as "Konino.". Two shrines with the same name exist about 500 {{ill|lt=meters|Q11573|qid=Q11573|1=Metre}} apart. Before land reclamation, the south branch of the {{ill|lt=Yasu River|Q8049935|qid=Q8049935|1=Yasu River}} flowing from the Yasu River to {{ill|lt=Lake Biwa|Q200239|qid=Q200239|1=Lake Biwa}} ran between them, and both stood on the left-bank levee, so they are thought to originate in river-related worship''Shikinaisha Chosa Hokoku''.. Both preserve traditions of being the Shikinaisha, but it is geographically and historically impossible to determine one as the sole original shrine; rather, it has been pointed out that the Engishiki "Kojino Shrine" may have consisted of both shrines, which may once have had close ritual ties. === Kojino Shrine (Kaihatsu) === {{神社 |名称 = Kojino Shrine |画像 = [[File:己爾乃神社 - panoramio.jpg|270px]]
Torii |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Shiga Prefecture|Q131358|qid=Q131358|1=Shiga Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Moriyama|Q857225|qid=Q857225|1=Moriyama, Shiga}} Sumoto-cho, Kaihatsu 1607 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|05|37.4|N|135|58|12.7|E|scale:25000}} |祭神 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Izanagi-no-Mikoto|1=Izanagi|qid=Q813858}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Izanami-no-Mikoto|1=Izanami|qid=Q682306}} |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}} candidate shrine / former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|qid=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |創建 = tradition: {{ill|lt=Tencho|Q1373138|qid=Q1373138|1=Tenchō}} 6 (829) |本殿 = {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|qid=Q210684|lt=one-bay nagare-zukuri|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |別名 = Kaihatsu no Kojino Shrine |札所等 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=May 5|Q2550|qid=Q2550|1=May 5}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Shiga|label=Kojino Shrine|coordinates= {{coord2|35|05|37.4|N|135|58|12.7|E|}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|05|37.4|N|135|58|12.7|E}}}} }} It is located in Kaihatsu, Sumoto-cho. From its location it is called "Kaihatsu no Kojino Shrine," but because a bridge called "Retsukeizu-bashi" once crossed the former south branch of the Yasu River nearby before reclamation, it was also called "Retsukeizu no Kojino Shrine". === Enshrined deities === It enshrines {{ill|lt=Izanagi|Q813858|qid=Q813858|lt=Izanagi-no-Mikoto|1=Izanagi}} and {{ill|lt=Izanami|Q682306|qid=Q682306|lt=Izanami-no-Mikoto|1=Izanami}} as principal deities, with {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Koyane|Q2564508|qid=Q2564508|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Koyane}} and {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ikatsuchi no Omi no Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} as associated deities. Formerly the two associated deities were the principal deities under the names '''{{ill|lt=Wakaichioji|Q11616186|qid=Q11616186|1=Nyakuichi Ōji}}''' (for Ame-no-Koyane) and '''{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Wakaichi Gongen|1=UNKNOWN}}''' (for Ikatsuchi-no-Omi), but on September 22, {{ill|lt=1881|Q7808|qid=Q7808|1=1881}} (Meiji 14), they were changed to the current arrangement. === Origin === Shrine tradition says it was founded in {{ill|lt=829|Q30357|qid=Q30357|1=829}} ({{ill|lt=Tencho|Q1373138|qid=Q1373138|1=Tenchō}} 6) by Iga Atsumu. Atsumu, also called "Taifu Atsumu," is said to be a descendant of the Iga clan, whose ancestor was Iga no Suketoyo. Since the Iga clan was related to the {{ill|lt=Nakatomi clan|Q1198447|qid=Q1198447|1=Nakatomi clan}}''{{ill|lt=Shinsen Shojiroku|Q1045362|qid=Q1045362|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku}}'', Sakyo Shinbetsu-jō., it is said they enshrined Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto and Ikatsuchi-no-Omi-no-Mikoto, ancestral deities of the Nakatomi clanShiga Prefecture Shrine Office, "[http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=463 Kojino Shrine]" (accessed Feb. 28, 2010).. Because it was often damaged by flooding of the Yasu River, only one volume titled ''Kojino Jinja no Engi-gaki'' remains, while treasures and old records were lost. There was also a shrine temple called {{ill|lt=Wakaichioji Temple (Moriyama)|UNKNOWN|lt=Wakaichioji Temple}}, and present-day {{ill|lt=Renko-ji (Moriyama)|UNKNOWN|lt=Renko-ji}} is said to continue its line. ''{{ill|lt=Omi Yochi Shiryaku|Q85883966|qid=Q85883966|1=Omi Yochi Shiryaku}}'' records a {{ill|lt=Yakushi Hall|Q11622438|qid=Q11622438|1=Bhaiṣajyaguru Hall}} near the shrine with a Buddhist image said to be made by Dengyo Daishi ({{ill|lt=Saicho|Q449943|qid=Q449943|1=Saichō}}), and this is inferred to have housed the shrine's {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=honji-butsu|1=UNKNOWN}}. Later it received a Senior First Rank {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sogen Senji|1=UNKNOWN}} from the {{ill|lt=Yoshida family|Q11413722|qid=Q11413722|1=Yoshida family}} of the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Jingi Kanryo|1=UNKNOWN}}, and still preserves a sacred mirror inscribed "Senior First Rank Wakaichi Daigongen". Its former rank was {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|qid=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. === Festivals === * Annual festival - May 5 === Precinct shrine === * Kojino Wakamiya === Kojino Shrine (Nakanomae) === {{神社 |名称 = Kojino Shrine |画像 = [[File:Kojino-jinja (Nakanomae) 202202-1.jpg|270px]]
Torii |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Shiga Prefecture|Q131358|qid=Q131358|1=Shiga Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Moriyama|Q857225|qid=Q857225|1=Moriyama, Shiga}} Sumoto-cho, Nakanomae 2322 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|05|42.0|N|135|57|56.7|E|scale:25000}} |祭神 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōanamuji-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Amatsuhikone-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}
{{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qid=Q272993|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}} candidate shrine / former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|qid=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |創建 = tradition: {{ill|lt=Taiho|Q423370|qid=Q423370|1=Taiho}} 3 (703) |本殿 = {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|qid=Q210684|lt=one-bay nagare-zukuri|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |別名 = Ōmagari no Kojino Shrine |札所等 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=May 5|Q2550|qid=Q2550|1=May 5}} |神事 = Naming ritual |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Shiga|label=Kojino Shrine|coordinates= {{coord2|35|05|42.0|N|135|57|56.7|E|}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|05|42.0|N|135|57|56.7|E}}}} }} Located in Nakanomae, Sumoto-cho. From its location it is called "Nakanomae no Kojino Shrine" and "Ōmagari no Kojino Shrine" (Ōmagari was the old local area name adjacent to Nakanomae). === Enshrined deities === It enshrines {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|qid=Q276944|lt=Ōanamuji-no-Mikoto|1=Ōkuninushi}}, {{ill|lt=Amatsuhikone|Q10940228|qid=Q10940228|lt=Amatsuhikone-no-Mikoto|1=Amatsuhikone}}, and {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|qid=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}. === Origin === Tradition says it was founded by {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Nakatomi Tamee|1=UNKNOWN}} in {{ill|lt=703|Q23500|qid=Q23500|1=703}} ({{ill|lt=Taiho (Japan)|Q1346269|qid=Q1346269|lt=Taiho|1=Taihō (era)}} 3). Though shrine buildings were lost in warfare during the {{ill|lt=Eiroku|Q1143341|qid=Q1143341|1=Eiroku}} era (1558-1570), they were restored by {{ill|lt=1791|Q7784|qid=Q7784|1=1791}} ({{ill|lt=Kansei|Q1195131|qid=Q1195131|1=Kansei}} 3). Nearby {{ill|lt=Gyokurin-ji (Moriyama)|UNKNOWN|lt=Gyokurin-ji}} was formerly its shrine temple. Its former rank was village shrine. === Festivals === * Annual festival - May 5 === Precinct shrines === * Inari Shrine * Tenmangu * Ōshiro Shrine === Cultural property === * Bronze sutra cylinder - height 25.3 {{ill|lt=cm|Q174728|qid=Q174728|1=Centimetre}}, diameter 11.2 cm. A {{ill|lt=bronze|Q753|qid=Q753|1=Copper}} {{ill|lt=sutra container|Q11606503|qid=Q11606503|1=kyōzutsu}} from the {{ill|lt=Muromachi period|Q334845|qid=Q334845|1=Muromachi period}}. Designated as Moriyama City cultural property (craft) on August 26, {{ill|lt=1974|Q2478|qid=Q2478|1=1974}} (Showa 49). === Notes === {{reflist}} === References === * ''Shikinaisha Chosa Hokoku'', Vol.12 Tōsandō 1, {{ill|lt=Kogakkan University|UNKNOWN|lt=Kogakkan University Press}}, 1981 === External links === *[http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=463 Kojino Shrine] (Kaihatsu) (Shiga Prefecture Shrine Office) *[http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=464 Kojino Shrine] (Nakanomae) (Shiga Prefecture Shrine Office) {{神道 横}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Buddhism-stub}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{pref-stub|pref=滋賀県}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kojino Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kojino Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kojino Shrine]][[Category:Disputed Shikinaisha or Shikigeisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ōmi Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shiga Prefecture]] [[Category:近江国の式内社]] [[Category:滋賀県の旧村社]] [[Category:Municipal History of Moriyama]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Moriyama, Shiga]] [[Category:熊野信仰]]
